national_expectations.summary: Algeria arrived in North America chasing a return to the knockout rounds for the first time since their Round-of-16 run in Brazil in 2014, ending a 12-year World Cup absence. Coverage frames reaching the last 16 as the clear public objective, with a squad rated among Africa's strongest carrying ambitions to re-establish the nation among the world's elite. Recognized previews cast this as a defining tournament for captain Riyad Mahrez, who has said the 2026 finals will be his last, blended with an emerging generation including Ibrahim Maza, Luca Zidane, Amine Gouiri and Anis Hadj Moussa. Having now advanced from the group, the brief shifts to proving the current generation can write its own chapter.

national_mood.summary: The mood lifted sharply after a chaotic 3-3 draw with Austria sealed progression, a result widely described in the French- and English-language press as 'crazy' and beyond the limits of endurance. Earlier in the group, after the opening loss to Argentina, the narrative was one of pressure and relief: the comeback win over Jordan was framed as substitutes rescuing Algeria and easing the pressure on the coach. Reaching the knockout phase is reported against the painful backdrop of the 2022 'Blida' playoff failure that kept Algeria out of Qatar, an episode national and continental outlets still treat as a generational wound the squad wants to put behind it.

manager_situation.summary: Vladimir Petković, the Bosnian-Swiss coach appointed in February 2024, steadied a tense campaign and emerged with his standing strengthened. After the Argentina defeat some observers had questioned his future, but his in-game substitutions in the Jordan win, and the dramatic recovery against Austria, were credited by the Algerian press as the difference. The Round of 32 carries an unusual subplot: Petković managed Switzerland (the reported Group B winner) between 2014 and 2021, making a potential reunion against his former national team an emotionally charged storyline; he said his immediate priority after Austria was rest and recovery before turning to the next round. He publicly insisted Algeria would always try to win rather than manage results.

historical_context.summary: 2026 is Algeria's fifth World Cup (after 1982, 1986, 2010 and 2014). Their best run remains the Round of 16 in Brazil 2014, where they pushed eventual champions Germany to extra time. Algeria's tournament debut in 1982 produced a famous 2-1 win over reigning champions West Germany, but that edition is also remembered for the 'Disgrace of Gijón', when a tepid West Germany-Austria result eliminated Algeria, a parallel the press revived ahead of the 2026 Austria fixture. Per the authoritative group result, Algeria finished third in Group J with four points (loss to Argentina, win over Jordan, draw with Austria) and advanced as one of the best third-placed teams.

dominant_storylines.storyline: Ending the 12-year wait: reaching the knockout stage for the first time since the celebrated 2014 Round-of-16 run in Brazil is the headline achievement, framed against a generation haunted by the 2022 'Blida' playoff failure that kept Algeria out of Qatar.

The chaotic 3-3 with Austria and a revived 'Gijón 1982' collusion debate: with both teams able to advance, the dramatic late goals prompted match-fixing suspicions echoing the 1982 'Disgrace of Gijón'; Petković dismissed the collusion talk and insisted Algeria only tried to win.

Petković's reunion subplot: the coach who led Switzerland between 2014 and 2021 is reported to face his former team in the Round of 32, a poignant matchup he said he had not yet turned his attention to, prioritising rest before the next round.

Mahrez's reported final tournament and a regenerating squad: the captain's stoppage-time goal against Austria anchored a narrative of an experienced core (Mahrez, Bentaleb) blended with rising talents like Amine Gouiri, Ibrahim Maza, Luca Zidane and Anis Hadj Moussa, with Petković's substitutions repeatedly decisive.

National-press selection debate ('4 pieces of the puzzle'): Algerian/French-African coverage frames Petković as still juggling four unresolved choices before the Switzerland tie — the goalkeeper question (Oussama Benbot, recalled from retirement, erred against Austria, reopening debate with the earlier-criticised Luca Zidane), Houssem Aouar's claim to a midfield berth after his two assists set up Mahrez, the left-flank call between an injury-doubtful Mohamed Amoura and the harder-working Farès Chaïbi, and how to stop the individual defensive errors that leaked goals across the group. Talk of reverting to a five-man defensive shape tested in friendlies is reported.

Petković's insider read as a 'battle in the shadows': Algerian outlet Algerie360 presents the coach's seven years building Switzerland (2014-2021) as a concrete analytical edge — he knows the Nati's methods, schemes and collective mechanisms — framing the tactical-preparation duel as competitive as the match itself, while portraying this Swiss side as a disciplined, cohesive unit that compensates for the absence of the old Shaqiri-era star power.

Forward-looking, respectful tone in the Algerian press after the Austria comeback: TSA's at-the-whistle reactions piece gathers the squad immediately pivoting to Switzerland rather than dwelling on the drama — Mahrez calling the next match the priority, Maza bracing for a complicated game, and Aouar voicing respect for a Swiss side that 'plays very well' — capturing a measured, focused mood inside the camp ahead of only Algeria's second-ever knockout appearance.

Mahrez frames qualification as merited, not lucky: in the Algerian Arabic-language press, the captain pushes back on any sense that the chaotic Austria draw flattered Algeria, insisting the side earned its passage through the level it produced and a fighting spirit — a message squarely aimed at a home audience still scarred by the 2022 Blida failure.

attributed_quotes.quote: I think the match was a little crazy. It sort of went beyond the limits of everyone's endurance.
attributed_quotes.context: Post-match press conference reaction to the 3-3 draw with Austria that sent Algeria into the Round of 32.

attributed_quotes.quote: There is no 'if' or 'but' in football, we have to face our opponents always the same way.
attributed_quotes.context: Pre-match, denying Algeria would deliberately draw with Austria to avoid drawing Spain in the knockout round.

attributed_quotes.quote: It's a solid block. I know their style of play, even if they've evolved.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: C'est un bloc solide. Je connais leur style de jeu, même s'ils ont évolué.
attributed_quotes.context: At-the-whistle reaction after the 3-3 with Austria, previewing the Round of 32 against Switzerland, the team he coached from 2014 to 2021.

attributed_quotes.quote: The most important thing is the next match [against Switzerland] and trying to qualify.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Le plus important, c'est le prochain match [contre la Suisse] et essayer de se qualifier.
attributed_quotes.context: Post-Austria reaction, pivoting from his stoppage-time goals to the knockout tie with Switzerland.

attributed_quotes.quote: We deserved to advance based on the level we showed.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: كنا نستحق العبور بالنظر إلى المستوى الذي قدمناه
attributed_quotes.context: Mahrez rejecting any suggestion the dramatic Austria draw flattered Algeria's passage to the knockout phase.

local_availability_reporting.status: No injuries or suspensions were reported for Algeria ahead of their final group match against Austria, with Mahrez and Gouiri reported in the starting XI; no R32-specific availability concerns were found in credible reporting as of 2026-06-28.

source_data_snapshot.note: Algeria World Cup history (1982/1986/2010/2014, R16 best, West Germany 2-1 in 1982); names Mahrez.
Petković quote re-verified verbatim; Gijón parallel; reports Algeria 3rd faces Switzerland.
3-3, Mahrez stoppage-time goals; states Austria runners-up, Algeria THIRD (consistent with DB).
Jordan 2-1 win, substitutions (Benbouali, Hadj Moussa, Gouiri), manager-pressure narrative; does NOT label Algeria runners-up.
2022 Blida trauma framing; Belmadi/Romano quotes relate to 2022, excluded from current-mood quotes.
Expectations, 2014 legacy, names Maza/Zidane/Gouiri/Hadj Moussa/Bentaleb; does NOT frame Mahrez as farewell.
Petković hired Feb 29 2024; ex-Switzerland coach ~7 years through Euro 2020.
Petković managed Switzerland 2014-2021; Algeria face Switzerland in R32; 'rest then begin again' framing.
Mahrez, 35, says 2026 is his last World Cup; 'last dance' framing (replaces unreachable Canary source).

source_data_snapshot.used_for: At-the-whistle Algerian-press reactions; verbatim French quotes re-verified for Petković ('bloc solide / je connais leur style'), Mahrez ('le prochain match... essayer de se qualifier') and Maza ('un match compliqué'); respectful forward-looking mood toward Switzerland.
Arabic-language Mahrez quote re-verified verbatim ('كنا نستحق العبور بالنظر إلى المستوى الذي قدمناه'); merited-qualification framing for a home audience.

National-press 'battle in the shadows' (la bataille dans l'ombre) tactical-knowledge storyline; Petković's 2014-2021 Swiss tenure as analytical edge; confirmed NO verbatim Petković quotes in piece (author analysis).

'4 pieces of the puzzle' R32 selection-debate storyline (Benbot/Zidane GK, Aouar role, Amoura injury vs Chaïbi, defensive errors, possible 5-man shape); fills the file's flagged R32 team-news gap; confirmed no attributed verbatim quotes.

gaps: DB FACTS list the R32 opponent as TBD, but multiple credible outlets (ESPN, Sky Sports, geosuper.tv) report Algeria will face Switzerland (reported Group B winner) in Vancouver on/around July 2, 2026. Per the grounding contract the DB is authoritative, so next_opponent_code is kept TBD; flag for review once the bracket is DB-confirmed.

Group position is DB-consistent across sources: Sky Sports, ESPN and the DB all have Algeria third in Group J with four points (Austria runners-up on goal difference), advancing as a best third-placed team. The earlier draft note that Sky Sports/Footmercato 'mislabel Algeria as runners-up' was incorrect and has been removed.

No credible quote found from within Algeria/the federation directly about the R32 tie itself (most quotes are pre/post the Austria group game); FAF (Fédération Algérienne de Football) official statements not located.
No reporting located on the domestic mood inside Algeria specifically after R32 qualification (Algiers street reaction); only pre-tournament framing and the Blida backdrop were found.
No confirmed R32 team news (injuries, suspensions, rotation) located as of 2026-06-28; availability reporting is limited to the pre-Austria group fixture.

The Canary (thecanary.co) cited in the original draft now returns HTTP 403 and could not be re-verified; its expectations/'last dance' framing was reassigned to re-fetched OneFootball and CAF sources.
Eurosport 'Blida' quotes (Belmadi, Romano) relate to the 2022 playoff trauma, not the current tournament, so they were excluded from attributed_quotes to avoid mis-dating current mood.

Petković's reported 'not yet had time to process' the Switzerland reunion appears in wire summaries; directly-fetched articles (OneFootball, geosuper.tv) show the milder 'rest then begin again' wording, so the storyline was softened to that verified framing.
